# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

cluster:
  name: elasticsearch_production
node:
  name: "{{hostname}}"
  master: true
  data: false
  ingest: true
bootstrap:
  memory_lock: true
  ulimits:
    memlock:
      enabled: true
      max: 2684354560 # equals to 256GB
      soft_limit: 17179869184 # equals to 160GB, adjust as needed. The soft limit is for situations where your system has low virtual memory. It must be less than the `hard_limit` value. If set too high it can cause problems with the Elasticsearch process.
    nofile:
      enabled: true
      max: 65536 # maximum number of open file descriptors, usually equal to number of CPUs multiplied by 64 for optimal performance. Adjust as required
path:
  logs: /var/log/elasticsearch
network:
  host: 0.0.0.0   # Elasticsearch listens on all IP addresses
http:
  enabled: true
  compression: true
  cors:
    enabled: true
    allow-origin: "*"
discovery:
  seed_hosts: ["node1", "node2"]
  cluster_name: elasticsearch_production
  bootstrap_memory: true
  type: single_node
xpack:
  security:
    enabled: true # You should always enable security. See https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/security-minimal-setup.html for details
